overview.text: Zhipu AI, also branded Z.ai, is a Beijing AI company founded in 2019 as a spin-out from Tsinghua University research led by Tang Jie and Li Juanzi, known for its GLM family of language models, many released open-weight.

what_theyve_done.text: Zhipu's researchers published the original GLM training algorithm in 2022, then shipped GLM-4-Plus in August 2024 and an end-to-end speech model, GLM-4-Voice, that October.
It released GLM-4.5 and GLM-4.5 Air in July 2025, GLM-4.6 running on domestic Chinese chips that September, and GLM-5 in February 2026 followed by the open-source GLM-5.1 that April.
In June 2026 it released GLM-5.2, a roughly 753-billion-parameter mixture-of-experts model with a stable 1-million-token context window built around a new IndexShare sparse-attention mechanism.

citations.quote: reducing per-token FLOPs by 2.9× at a 1M context length

strengths.text: GLM-5.2 sustains a solid 1-million-token context for long-horizon work and offers multiple thinking-effort levels to balance coding performance against latency.
It is released under the MIT license with, per Zhipu's own description, no regional restrictions on access.

citations.quote: no regional limits, technical access without borders

current_focus.text: Zhipu's current focus with GLM-5.2 is engineering reliability for long coding-agent workflows -- large-scale implementation, automated research, performance optimization and complex debugging -- over raw token-context capacity alone, including building anti-hacking mechanisms into its reinforcement-learning training to stop agents from gaming evaluations.

citations.quote: large-scale implementation, automated research, performance optimization
